title: Assets
subtitle: viewer, tools, backlog, generation skill
description: AI Studio asset modules decomposed into the viewer surface, concrete asset tools, the image-generation skill, and a temporary backlog for storage/source-registry pieces that still need a cleaner split.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Assets module contract: current ownership for viewer, tools, backlog storage, source registration, preview cache, license guard, and the image-generation skill boundary.

children.title: Asset Viewer
children.subtitle: visual asset surface
children.description: Browser-facing asset viewer surface for library browsing, game-asset review, pick manifests, 3D model inspection, promotion, and reuse pulls. It consumes backlog storage but does not own storage or registry data.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Asset Viewer module contract: modes, commands, ownership boundaries, and why the gallery server remains separate.

children.title: Gallery Entry
children.subtitle: source picker inside gallery
children.description: Asset Viewer entry hosted at /viewer/. The gallery itself lists available sources and switches between all assets, template assets, and the current game.

children.title: index.html
children.description: Asset Viewer browser entry. It loads the existing gallery UI and lets the user choose the displayed asset source from inside the gallery header.

children.title: api.mjs
children.description: Studio Shell API adapter for listing asset sources, opening the selected source in the gallery, rebuilding/querying the asset index, and resolving gallery media.

children.title: api.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for Asset Viewer API source selection and confined gallery/library/repo media path resolution.

children.title: build_review.mjs
children.description: Generates static asset gallery outputs for shared-library browsing, game-asset review, or direct asset-folder scans.

children.title: serve_gallery.mjs
children.description: Serves a generated asset gallery and maps a large external asset library at /lib/ so library browsing can avoid copying every media file.

children.title: serve_gallery.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for standalone Asset Viewer gallery server argument parsing and safe gallery/library request path resolution.

children.title: promote.mjs
children.description: Promotes selected assets from a review manifest into Pack Manifest storage with license records and integrity data.

children.title: pull.mjs
children.description: Copies selected shared-library assets into a game-local asset tree while preserving source_id linkage and restricted-asset routing.

children.title: pull.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for Asset Viewer pull argument parsing and preservation of publishability metadata in game-local manifest rows.

children.title: record_gallery.mjs
children.description: Records a browser session of the asset gallery for review or presentation evidence.

children.title: record_gallery.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for Asset Viewer recorder argument parsing and gallery hero selection without launching Chrome.

children.title: Surface Implementation
children.subtitle: gallery js/css
children.description: Static browser files for the Asset Viewer gallery. Hidden from the default graph so the viewer module remains readable as one surface.

children.title: viewer.js
children.description: Vanilla browser SPA for filters, pack views, asset modal, picking, and model-viewer integration.

children.title: viewer.css
children.description: Asset Viewer page styling for gallery cards, filters, sheets, and model previews.

children.title: Tests
children.subtitle: asset viewer regressions
children.description: Regression tests for Asset Viewer generation and promotion behavior.

children.title: build_review.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for asset kind classification, origin detection, escaping, and gallery HTML rendering.

children.title: promote.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for promoting picked assets into Pack Manifest storage with license and integrity records.

children.title: nt-asset-image-generation
children.description: Raster generation skill used only after source-first search fails. It is the generation entrypoint; asset tools handle file preparation and games own any game-specific prompt/provenance records.

children.title: generation-paths.md
children.description: Exact image generation command routes for Codex imagegen, fallback comparison mode, and REST/backend alternatives.

children.title: verification-and-prompts.md
children.description: Prompt and verification guidance for real raster generation, fake-output detection, composable asset prompts, and known dead ends.

children.title: throughput-and-handoff.md
children.description: Batch generation, source-sheet-first guidance, skip sidecars, and handoff back to asset workflow and quality checks.

children.title: Generation Scripts
children.description: Executable wrappers for image generation, compare mode, batch generation, and dual-plate soft-alpha generation.

children.title: codex_imagegen.sh
children.description: Shell wrapper that forces Codex CLI imagegen to create and write a real raster PNG, with fake-output size checks.

children.title: generate_image.py
children.description: Python image generation/edit helper using official REST with API key or Codex backend credentials, plus skip-if-unchanged sidecars.

children.title: gen_batch.py
children.description: Concurrent batch runner for multiple image generation jobs with dry-run, bounded concurrency, and unchanged-job skipping.

children.title: gen_both.sh
children.description: Compare-mode runner that generates the same prompt through Codex imagegen and Antigravity fallback for visual selection.

children.title: gen_dual_plate.sh
children.description: Soft-alpha dual-plate generation helper used by cutout prep when one flat-key source cannot recover fractional alpha.

children.title: Asset Tools
children.subtitle: crop, alpha, conversion, source sheets
children.description: Concrete utilities for asset work: source-sheet checks, crop planning, alpha/cutout cleanup, conversion, texture checks, manual local prep UI, and step-local verification outputs.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Asset Tools contract: what asset tools own, what they do not own, and the current source-sheet, crop, cutout, review-atlas, and texture groups.

children.title: Tools Library
children.subtitle: shared python helpers
children.description: Low-level Python helpers shared by asset asset tools. Not a public workflow surface.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Tools Library contract: small shared helpers for tools tools only.

children.title: atomic_io.py
children.description: Atomic text, JSON, file-copy, and image-save helpers used by Python asset tools tools.

children.title: Image Tools
children.subtitle: media-type umbrella: sourcing, bg, regions, slice, alpha, route
children.description: Media-type umbrella for raster image work, decomposed into per-tool folders (sourcing, background normalization, region detection, slicing, key-matte and dual-plate alpha, routing) over a shared _bridge. Peer tier to future model3d/audio tool umbrellas; runs against a repo-local studio Python venv resolved from studio.config.json pythonPath.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Image Tools umbrella contract: per-tool decomposition, the studio Python venv, and the shared _bridge.

children.title: _bridge
children.description: Shared image-tool plumbing: config-only Python interpreter resolution, tmp-path confinement, session dirs, JSON/image helpers, and the one-shot venv setup script. Not a tool.

children.title: alpha_matte
children.description: Single-background known-key cutout (path 1) for opaque art and flat-key holes: key_matte.py plus the chroma_key_alpha.py keying primitive and their tests.

children.title: alpha_dualplate
children.description: Fractional alpha (glow, glass, soft shadow) from an aligned white/black plate pair (path 2): dual_plate_alpha.py Smith & Blinn projection plus the pair-consistency gate and tests.

children.title: route
children.description: Chooses key-matte vs dual-plate for a flat-key source crop from soft-alpha risk metrics before a generation is spent.

children.title: bg_fix
children.description: Border-connected background chroma normalization: snap the source's border-connected background to the key colour without changing interior key-coloured art.

children.title: regions
children.description: Region detection over a normalized source: find component rects, merge fragments, pad, and write a regions manifest plus an overlay.

children.title: slice
children.description: Slice a source by reviewed regions, apply per-region alpha (key-matte by default; no silent fallback), optionally polygon-mask, and write PNGs, a manifest, a review sheet, and a ZIP.

children.title: sources
children.description: Source-image upload conventions for the image tools: where draft/generated images live before they become accepted assets.

children.title: Conversion Tools
children.subtitle: format conversion
children.description: Conversion utilities that normalize source assets into inspectable or importable formats.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Conversion Tools contract: file conversion without license, storage, or runtime-loading decisions.

children.title: obj_to_glb.py
children.description: Blender headless OBJ/MTL to GLB converter with split-by-material output for engine-friendly meshes.

children.title: Source Sheet Tools
children.subtitle: chroma and component audit
children.description: Tools for generated UI/icon/sprite/decor source sheets: normalize border-connected chroma background and audit component count, gutters, borders, and key-color conflicts before cropping.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Source Sheet Tools command guide for normalize_chroma.py and audit_intake.py.

children.title: normalize_chroma.py
children.description: Normalizes border-connected chroma background pixels in generated source sheets without changing interior key-colored art.

children.title: audit_intake.py
children.description: Audits a source sheet for component separation, unsafe borders/gutters, and key-color conflicts before crop planning.

children.title: source_sheets/tests
children.description: Regression tests for source-sheet chroma normalization and intake audit behavior.

children.title: Crop Tools
children.subtitle: source-sheet split plans
children.description: Tools for splitting source sheets into named asset regions and future manual crop-boundary editing.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Crop Tools contract: crop plans and future boundary-editing ownership.

children.title: plan_prepared_crops_from_intake.py
children.description: Creates named crop-plan entries from a passing source-sheet intake audit.

children.title: plan_prepared_crops_from_intake_test.py
children.description: Regression tests for named crop-plan generation.

children.title: Review Atlas
children.subtitle: visual contact sheets
children.description: Tools for building and auditing visual contact sheets used for source review. This is not engine integration output.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Review Atlas contract: visual contact sheets for inspection, not engine integration output.

children.title: build_review_atlas.py
children.description: Builds a labeled review atlas/contact sheet for prepared UI, icon, or sprite outputs.

children.title: audit_review_atlas.py
children.description: Audits review-atlas layout, labels, and source coverage.

children.title: Texture Tools
children.subtitle: tile preview and seam metrics
children.description: Tools for standalone material textures: generate 2x2 repeat previews and measure obvious edge mismatches before accepting a texture as tileable.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Texture Tools command guide for tileable texture seam audits and preview generation.

children.title: audit_tileable_texture.py
children.description: Writes a 2x2 texture preview and reports mean/max edge deltas for repeat-texture candidates.

children.title: audit_tileable_texture.test.py
children.description: Regression tests for tileable texture preview output and seam failure reporting.

children.title: Canvas
children.subtitle: multi-image projects, agent + browser parity
children.description: Figma/Recraft-like multi-image canvas projects. Every capability is one operation in a shared ops layer with two equal clients: the agent (CLI/import) and the thin browser page. Owns project persistence, an append-only operation journal with linear undo/redo, groups as named game screens (Figma-frame regions that own elements, hide/show, move as a whole, and export as one composited PNG), region slicing into new elements, and selected-element export; bridges detect_regions and slice to the image tools pipeline unmodified and composites screens with its own render_group.py tool.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Canvas module contract: owner/boundary, layout, projects-root resolution, the one operation layer, CLI usage, and validation commands.

children.title: PLAN.md
children.description: Lead-owned working plan for the canvas module: the non-negotiable laws (tool parity, thin page, non-destructive art), the increment order, design decisions, and the narrative state that the taskboard epic E010 tasks are scoped against.

children.title: store.mjs
children.description: Canvas project persistence: one folder per project with project.json (schema ai_studio.canvas.project.v1), a thin append-only journal.jsonl (op metadata only) with fat before/after snapshots in a sidecar snapshots/<seq>.json, and an immutable content-addressed files/. Atomic writes, path-confined ids/filenames/export paths, pure PNG/JPEG/GIF dimension parsing with persisted source_w/source_h, and lazy projects-root creation. Owns the journal plumbing: O(1) tail-read seq allocation, sidecar snapshot read/write/delete, thin-journal rewrite/archive, a transparent idempotent one-time migration of legacy fat journals (kept as journal.jsonl.bak), a capped tool_runs array with a tool_runs.jsonl spill, and the per-project errors.jsonl failure sink.

children.title: tree.mjs
children.description: Pure, dependency-free scene-tree math shared by ops.mjs (node) AND the site (static /ai_studio/ import), so paint order itself obeys tool parity — one implementation, two clients. Over the flat Defold-style model (elements[]/groups[] with additive optional group.parentId and per-node numeric order fields, no stored tree) it computes per-scope children (childrenOf), back-to-front z-order (orderedChildren: the explicit numeric order when every sibling has it, else a v1 fallback where an element keys on its elements[] index and a group anchors at its backmost subtree member so legacy projects paint unchanged, empty group to front), subtree collection (descendantsOf), the ancestor chain (ancestorsOf, cycle-capped), the visibility cascade (isNodeHidden: self or any ancestor group hidden), and the reparent cycle guard (wouldCycle). All downward/upward walks are cycle-safe and resolve a dangling/corrupt parent to root so a bad project never hangs or hides content.

children.title: ops.mjs
children.description: The one operation layer both clients call: journaled store wrappers (addImage/patchElement/removeElement/reorderElement), project-level patchProject (journaled rename; title carried in the snapshot) and deleteProject (moves the folder to the projects-root .trash, not journaled), group (screen) ops (createGroup/patchGroup/assignToGroup/deleteGroup) whose metadata-only snapshots also carry groups so undo restores frames and members together, linear undo/redo/history over the thin sidecar-snapshot journal, the bridged detectRegions and sliceRegions (which reuse the image tools upload/detect/slice functions unmodified; setRegions applies polygon validation and bbox derivation, and slice alpha-masks polygonal regions), the Figma-style export surface (setExportSettings journals per-element export rows [{scale, suffix, format, quality?, resample}] with resolveExportScale/parseScaleSpec token parsing; exportElements scales+encodes each element x row via one tools/export_images.py spawn with a byte-identical 1x-png Node copy fast path; exportProject composites every visible screen at 1x png into one folder reusing the renderGroup compositor), and renderGroup (spawns tools/render_group.py to composite a screen PNG). reorderElement is the z-order op: it moves an element to a target index among its siblings (root or its group), permuting only that sibling subsequence within the flat array slots it occupies (array order IS paint/composite order everywhere), clamps the index, and journals one undoable entry. commitMutation writes fat snapshots to a sidecar + a thin duration_ms line and compacts history past canvasHistoryDepth (default 200) with a clean undo horizon; observability adds duration_ms on every entry, recordOpFailure into errors.jsonl, and an opsStats timing rollup. Records regions, slice crops with provenance, and capped tool_runs entries on the project.

children.title: api.mjs
children.description: Studio Shell HTTP adapter for canvas projects (list/create/get/rename via PATCH/delete-to-trash via DELETE), elements, base64 image upload with optional drop x/y, region detection, region slicing, element z-order (POST elements/<eid>/reorder {index}), per-element export settings (PUT elements/<eid>/export {rows}), export (POST export {elementIds, rows?} for elements or {project:true} for every visible screen), group create/patch/assign/delete and screen render, undo/redo/history, an ops-stats timing rollup route, confined image-file serving, and a confined export-file download route (export/<stamp>/<file>) under the /api/canvas/ prefix. Mutating responses add a duration_ms field (existing fields untouched) and project-resolvable failures are logged to the project errors.jsonl via recordOpFailure. Marshals HTTP to the ops layer only.

children.title: cli.mjs
children.description: Agent client over the same ops layer: list, create, show, rename, delete, add-image, detect-regions, move, element-set/remove, element-reorder (z-order among siblings), slice, export-set (persist per-element export rows), export (--elements/--all/--project with inline --scale/--format/--quality/--suffix/--resample override rows and a --to destination copy), group-create/move/set/assign/delete, render-screen, undo, redo, history, and ops-stats commands printing compact JSON. Project-resolvable failures are logged to the project errors.jsonl (mirrors the API adapter).

children.title: Canvas Tools
children.subtitle: screen compositor
children.description: The canvas module's own Python tools (not image-tools steps), spawned directly by the ops layer with the shared Python discovery.

children.title: render_group.py
children.description: PIL compositor for renderGroup: reads a render spec JSON (absolute element paths, group bounds, scale, background), alpha-composites the visible member elements in z-order clipped to the group box, and writes one screen PNG plus a JSON report atomically.

children.title: export_images.py
children.description: PIL scale+encode tool for exportElements: reads one export spec JSON (a flat list of jobs, each an absolute source path, output path, explicit target pixels, format, quality, resample) and encodes the whole batch in one spawn — png (RGBA), jpg (flattened onto white, quality), webp (quality) — with lanczos/nearest resampling; writes each file plus a JSON report atomically. The scale math lives in the JS op (resolveExportScale) and byte-identical 1x-png copies never reach this tool. Uses the T0218 _bridge config-only Python.

children.title: Canvas Page
children.subtitle: thin local interface
children.description: Thin Figma/Recraft-like browser page served via the /ai_studio/ static route. One HTML document swaps a home view (project card grid + inline new/rename/delete) and a workspace view (DPR-crisp pan/zoom canvas, left tool rail, collapsible group-aware layers panel, right inspector, right-click context menu, OS drag&drop + clipboard paste, zoom controls). Feedback is a Figma-like toast stack (there is no status bar): transient success/info, persistent errors, and pinned export/render results with download links, plus a busy spinner + client-side FIFO limiter for long python-backed ops. Behavior is split into small ES modules; every action is one HTTP API call and there is no logic beyond rendering/input.

children.title: canvas.html
children.description: Single page document holding both the home (project card grid) and workspace (top bar, tool rail, layers panel, stage, inspector) views that the controller swaps between. Feedback is a toast stack appended at runtime (no status bar element).

children.title: canvas.css
children.description: Dark-theme styling for the home card grid, workspace top bar, tool rail, layers panel (incl. the collapsed slim rail + reorder drop-line and styled scrollbars), stage overlays, inspector (titled collapsible sections + stable-gutter region list), the bottom-right toast/feedback stack (kind accents, CSS spinner, download links), inline editors, and context menu.

children.title: canvas.js
children.description: Page controller: boots the feature modules, owns view routing (deep link ?project=, last-opened localStorage restore, ?select= debug hook) and the global keyboard shortcuts, including z-order by physical key (event.code so any layout works): Ctrl+] / Ctrl+[ nudge a single selected element forward/backward and Ctrl+Alt+] / Ctrl+Alt+[ send it to front/back. No rendering/business logic of its own.

children.title: app.js
children.description: Shared page state, the fetch API helper, read-only view helpers over project.json, the image cache, selection helpers, deep-link/localStorage plumbing, and a refresh bus every panel renders through.

children.title: actions.js
children.description: The one place UI intents become a single HTTP API call: add/drop/paste image, patch/rename/hide/delete element, z-order (reorderElementTo + bring-forward/backward/to-front/to-back that translate the Figma intent into a sibling index for the one reorder op), detect/slice, export (setExportRows persists per-element rows; exportElementIds/exportProjectAction run the export op then deliver via the export_dest dir picker), group create/patch/render/ungroup/delete, undo/redo, and rename project. Panels never call the API directly.

children.title: home.js
children.description: Home view: a full-page grid of project cards (cover thumbnail, title, image count, updated date) plus a + New project card with an inline title input, and hover rename/delete.

children.title: workspace.js
children.description: Workspace view: the devicePixelRatio-crisp pan/zoom canvas (imageSmoothing off at >=2x), the tool rail (Select/Hand), zoom controls + indicator, top bar sync, group frame drawing, the region-edit tool row (Select/Rect/Polygon) with live polygon-draft drawing, and all pointer interaction (select, drag-move, pan with tool/Space/middle-mouse).

children.title: layers_panel.js
children.description: Group-aware layers list of ELEMENTS and GROUPS only (the region list lives in the inspector now): ungrouped elements at top level, groups as collapsible sections with eye toggle and inline-rename name, indented member rows with 24px thumbnail, region-count badge, and eye toggle; selection syncs both ways with the canvas. Render is structure-signature guarded so a selection-only change updates classes in place instead of rebuilding the DOM (keeps a row stable across a double-click, so dblclick rename works). Row drag reorders among siblings (a between-rows drop shows an insertion line and calls the reorder op) or reparents (drop onto a screen header / other group / top level). The panel collapses to a slim ~34px rail with a visible re-open control; collapsed state persists in localStorage.

children.title: inspector.js
children.description: Right inspector for the selection, organized into titled collapsible sections (Figma Design-tab style) whose collapsed state persists in localStorage per section: single element (name; Position & Size with source; a compact Regions section — count badge, per-region rows with rename/select/delete and a generous always-present delete target, plus Edit/Detect/+Add and Slice; Provenance; Meta; and a Figma-style Export section at the BOTTOM — a collapsible list of rows [scale + suffix + format, a quality slider only for jpg/webp, a resample toggle], + Add export setting, a destination hint, and a target-labeled Export button committing per-element export rows through setExportSettings), group/screen (name; Position & Size with visible + member count; Render screen with scale + background), multi-select (count + each-exports-its-own-settings + Export), or an empty state with a project-export button when there are visible screens. Render is structure-signature guarded so a region-selection-only change re-applies selection classes in place rather than rebuilding, keeping region rows stable across a double-click so dblclick rename opens its editor. The region list scrolls with a stable gutter + styled scrollbar (no hover layout shift).

children.title: context_menu.js
children.description: Right-click context menu whose items depend on the target (element / region / group label / empty canvas); every item calls an existing action and the menu closes on click-away or Escape. The element menu is a short list of frequent object actions — Edit regions, an Order submenu (bring forward/backward, to front/back → the reorder op), Move to screen / Group, Delete (no Rename or Hide; those are double-click and the layers eye dot; detect/slice and export live in the inspector).

children.title: dnd.js
children.description: OS drag & drop (drop images at the drop point with a drop highlight, offsetting multiples) and Ctrl/Cmd+V clipboard paste at the viewport center, over the shared actions.

children.title: inline.js
children.description: Inline text-editing helper: swaps a label for a text input that commits on Enter/blur and cancels on Escape, used to rename projects, elements, and groups without a browser prompt().

children.title: export_dest.mjs
children.description: Export destination delivery (delivery-to-disk only, an allowed page concern): the File System Access directory picker opens on every export starting at the last-used folder for the project (a handle remembered per project in IndexedDB via startIn), stores the new choice after each export, treats picker-cancel as a canceled export (no fallback), and falls back to real per-file browser downloads only when the File System Access API is unavailable — never silently dropping files. Fetches the exported bytes from the confined server download route.

children.title: toasts.js
children.description: Feedback layer that replaced the bottom status bar: a fixed bottom-right toast stack (clear of the inspector Export button + the breadcrumb chips) with kinds success/info (auto-hide ~3s, hover pauses), error (persists with op name + message, never swallowed), and pinned-result (export/render outcome with download links, persists). Toasts never block input (container pointer-events:none, cards only) and the stack caps at ~5, dropping the oldest transient toast first. Also hosts runLongOp: it wraps a python-backed op (detect/slice/render/export) with the long_op_queue limiter, shows a queued/running progress toast that resolves in place into the result, and disables the triggering control while in flight. Import-safe in node (no DOM at module load). setStatus/setStatusLinks in app.js are thin shims into this layer.

children.title: long_op_queue.mjs
children.description: Pure, DOM-free FIFO concurrency limiter for the page's long (python-backed) ops — detect/slice/render/export. Caps concurrent runs at N=2 so one browser never spawns a swarm of Python processes; extra requests queue and run FIFO as slots free, a failed op frees its slot, and a still-queued op can be cancelled before it fires (a running op cannot — no server cancellation). In-memory only: a page reload clears the queue. A page concern only (the CLI/ops path is unlimited). Node-unit-tested (slot accounting, FIFO order, failure-frees-slot, cancel-queued); toasts.js wraps it for the UI.

children.title: regions.js
children.description: Region workbench geometry: convert region rects and polygon rings to world/screen boxes, hit-test region bodies and resize handles (even-odd point-in-polygon for polygonal regions), map vertices via transformPolygon, and draw the bright numbered overlays. Pure DOM-free helpers for the workspace; region edits still persist through the shared setRegions op.

children.title: viewport.mjs
children.description: Pure canvas viewport math shared by the site modules (workspace, actions, dnd, regions): screen<->image point conversion, fit-to-frame centering, and zoom-at-cursor. No DOM and no rect-editing helpers (the region workbench owns rect/polygon geometry). The canvas owns its own viewport code here.

children.title: tests
children.description: Regression tests for the canvas store round-trip and confinement, deleteProject trash-move, studio config resolution incl. canvasHistoryDepth, the operation journal with undo/redo and redo-tail invalidation, journaled patchProject rename, element export folder/manifest, group create-from-elements bbox, group move + member translation with undo, delete-keeps-elements, visibility, renderGroup composite pixel checks via a tiny PNG decoder, the z-order reorderElement op (root + group sibling reorder, index clamp, interleaved-scope preservation, one-entry undo, and CLI element-reorder + API reorder-route parity), the HTTP API lifecycle plus rename/delete/undo/slice/export/group/render/reorder routes and the confined export-download route (rejecting traversal), the T0200 perf pass (batched patchElements/removeElements = one journal entry + single-undo restore with atomic validation, mutation responses folding canUndo/canRedo/seq so the page needs no follow-up GET, and immutable Cache-Control + ETag on the content-addressed files route), a CLI smoke incl. group + ops-stats + batched elements-set/elements-remove commands, the bridged detectRegions/sliceRegions/renderGroup ops (skips cleanly when the Python pipeline is unavailable), and the journal restructure: thin sidecar-snapshot lines, tail-read seq, idempotent fat-journal migration, history-depth compaction with a clean undo horizon, tool_runs cap/spill, duration_ms, and the errors.jsonl failure sink, plus the page's pure long-op FIFO limiter (slot accounting at max 2, FIFO order, a failed op freeing its slot, and cancel of a still-queued op).

children.title: Asset Backlog
children.subtitle: needs decomposition
children.description: Temporary holding area for asset modules that are functional but not yet cleanly decomposed into viewer or tools.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Asset Backlog contract: temporary home for live asset modules that need a clearer ownership split.

children.title: Backlog Storage
children.subtitle: temporary storage backlog
children.description: Functional storage/source-registry layer kept in backlog until it is decomposed into clearer ownership groups. It currently owns library registry data, source helpers, intake, manifests, indexes, snapshots, preview cache, and license policy.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Backlog Storage contract: source helpers, pack manifests, generated SQLite indexes, preview preparation, agent search, and boundary with Asset Viewer.

children.title: search.mjs
children.description: Agent-facing asset search command over generated SQLite indexes. Supports query, kind, tags, origin, license, pack, JSON output, and custom source paths.

children.title: kinds.mjs
children.description: Canonical mapping from asset kind to storage directory names used by manifests, viewer promotion, and storage/index readers.

children.title: search.test.mjs
children.description: Regression test proving agent asset search uses the generated index for a pack manifest source.

children.title: Asset Intake
children.subtitle: stage, accept, reject
children.description: Manifest-first intake for new asset candidates. Stages files with provenance, accepts selected files into packs or restricted packs, and rejects staged candidates without writing alternate metadata records.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Asset Intake contract: stage, review, accept or reject, Pack Manifest output, restricted-pack routing, and command examples.

children.title: stage.mjs
children.description: Stages a file, folder, zip, or URL into _incoming with hashes, source page, license hint, and provenance log.

children.title: accept.mjs
children.description: Accepts one staged file into Pack Manifest layout, routing publishable assets to packs and non-publishable assets to restricted/packs.

children.title: reject.mjs
children.description: Rejects a staged candidate by moving it into _rejected or deleting it explicitly.

children.title: intake.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for staging, manifest accept, restricted accept, and reject behavior.

children.title: Source Registry Helpers
children.subtitle: explicit asset roots
children.description: Helper modules and tests for explicit asset source roots. Global library registry data remains in backlog storage; template and game registry data lives with templates/ and games/.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Source Registry contract: why asset source roots are explicit and how global libraries, templates, and games are registered.

children.title: libraries.json
children.description: Explicit registry of global reusable asset libraries shown by Asset Viewer and used by asset CLI defaults.

children.title: libraries.mjs
children.description: Small helper for reading, listing, resolving, and upserting global library asset source records.

children.title: libraries.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for default library source listing, path resolution, and library source upserts.

children.title: templates.mjs
children.description: Small helper for reading, listing, and upserting template asset source records.

children.title: templates.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for default template source listing and template source upserts.

children.title: games.mjs
children.description: Small helper for reading, listing, and upserting game asset source records.

children.title: games.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for creating, listing, normalizing, and upserting registered game asset sources.

children.title: Pack Manifest
children.subtitle: pack.json + assets.jsonl
children.description: Preferred editable canonical format for reviewed asset packs. Stores pack metadata in pack.json and one asset record per line in assets.jsonl; generated SQLite indexes consume it for fast search.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Pack Manifest contract: layout, pack.json fields, assets.jsonl rows, path boundary, and current Asset Index integration.

children.title: manifest.mjs
children.description: Reads packs/<pack-id>/pack.json and assets.jsonl into normalized asset records and pack summaries for Asset Index.

children.title: manifest.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for pack.json/assets.jsonl reading, inherited pack metadata, relative paths, previews, and model paths.

children.title: Source Snapshots
children.subtitle: generated file diff layer
children.description: Generated file snapshot layer for asset refresh. Tracks added, changed, and deleted source files under tmp so Asset Index can skip unchanged rebuilds and later support incremental row updates.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Source Snapshots contract: generated snapshot location, tracked files, refresh consumers, and boundary with preview refresh.

children.title: snapshots.mjs
children.description: Builds source file snapshots, compact signatures, persisted generated snapshots, and added/changed/deleted diffs for asset refresh.

children.title: snapshots.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for source snapshot diffing and generated snapshot persistence.

children.title: Asset Index
children.subtitle: sqlite query layer
children.description: Local SQLite indexes, one per asset source, built from pack manifests or folder scans. Serves fast page, search, filter, pack, and model lookups for Asset Viewer and agent search without rescanning sources on each request.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Asset Index contract: one generated SQLite database per source, explicit rebuild/refresh, public query API, no watch mode, and boundary with Pack Manifest, folder scans, and Asset Viewer.

children.title: index.mjs
children.description: Builds and queries the local asset SQLite index from pack manifests or folder scans.
children.role: asset index API and rebuild helper

children.title: index.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for index rebuild, pack summary, search, filters, facets, paging, and model lookup.

children.title: License Guard
children.subtitle: public repo safety
children.description: Public-repository guard for paid, private, unknown-license, non-redistributable, and attribution-bearing asset binaries. Unknown licenses fail closed; described restricted assets remain local under assets/restricted/.

children.title: README.md
children.description: License Guard contract: publishable vs restricted binaries, CC-BY attribution metadata, public-repo scope, and validation commands.

children.title: restricted.mjs
children.description: Shared restricted-asset helper surface used by pull and guard: publishability, restricted root, attribution/notice checks, and binary extension classification.

children.title: registry.mjs
children.description: Canonical license decision registry for asset publishing: known licenses, custom-license defaults, attribution/notice requirements, and validation of publish decisions.

children.title: restricted_assets_guard.mjs
children.description: Validates git-tracked game/template asset binaries against manifest publishability and attribution metadata.

children.title: restricted_assets_exceptions.json
children.description: Empty-by-default exception list for already reviewed public binaries that cannot yet be manifested. New assets should be recorded in manifests instead of added here.

children.title: restricted_assets_guard.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for publishable assets, restricted routing, unregistered binaries, per-game paths, and CC-BY attribution metadata.

children.title: Preview Pipeline
children.subtitle: derived preview preparation
children.description: Local storage preparation for derived asset previews. Currently renders GLB thumbnails through Blender and writes previews back under previews/<asset-id>/.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Preview Pipeline contract: local preparation, Blender thumbnail generation, no watch mode, and viewer boundary.

children.title: cache.mjs
children.description: Source-aware preview cache refresh used by Asset Viewer. Copies or renders only missing/stale previews and refreshes the selected source index.

children.title: render_library_previews.mjs
children.description: Selects shared-library model assets and runs Blender thumbnail rendering in resumable chunks.

children.title: render_thumbs.py
children.description: Blender script that renders one isometric thumbnail per GLB using the shared studio HDR.

children.title: make_studio_hdr.py
children.description: Rebuilds the shared studio HDR used by Blender thumbnails and browser model-viewer lighting.

children.title: studio_env.hdr
children.description: Shared studio environment used by generated model thumbnails and live 3D model previews.

children.title: cache.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for missing, cached, and stale source-aware preview refresh.

children.title: nt-asset-workflow
children.description: Broad asset workflow skill kept in backlog while the asset architecture is split into viewer, tools, the image-generation skill, and storage/backlog responsibilities.

children.title: source-library-and-license.md
children.description: Reference for searching the asset library, pulling assets into games, promoting assets into the global library, and applying the license gate.

children.title: restricted-assets.md
children.description: Reference for paid, account-gated, unknown-license, and non-redistributable assets that must not commit binaries.

children.title: generated-assets-and-cutouts.md
children.description: Reference for generated source art, cutout/chroma cleanup, crop plans, slice9 metadata, and final-art boundaries.

children.title: 3d-models.md
children.description: Reference for model sourcing, conversion, texture deduplication, and game-local engine pack/load integration.

children.title: textures-and-materials.md
children.description: Reference for standalone texture/material sourcing, tiling decisions, material maps, downloaded texture provenance, and seam checks.
children.role: texture and material asset workflow reference
